title: Real-World Gallery
subtitle: Factory pattern in production code you use every day

examples.title: Java Calendar.getInstance()
examples.description: Returns a GregorianCalendar, JapaneseImperialCalendar, or BuddhistCalendar based on locale. You never call 'new GregorianCalendar()' directly.
examples.lesson: Even the JDK uses Factory to hide which Calendar implementation you get.

examples.title: NumberFormat.getInstance()
examples.description: Returns DecimalFormat, CompactNumberFormat, or other formatters based on locale and style. Same Factory pattern.
examples.lesson: Factory + Locale = automatic regional behavior without if-else.

examples.title: Spring BeanFactory & ApplicationContext
examples.description: Spring's entire IoC container IS a massive Factory. getBean() is create(). @Component is registration. Autowiring is injection.
examples.lesson: If you've used Spring, you've used Factory pattern every day.

examples.title: SLF4J LoggerFactory
examples.description: LoggerFactory.getLogger() returns Logback, Log4j2, or JUL logger based on classpath. Classic Factory.
examples.lesson: Factory enables framework-agnostic code. Your app doesn't know which logger it uses.

examples.title: JDBC DriverManager
examples.description: DriverManager.getConnection() returns MySQL, PostgreSQL, or Oracle connection based on URL. Pure Factory with Registry pattern.
examples.lesson: JDBC DriverManager is a Registry-based Factory. Add a JAR = new database support.

examples.title: React.createElement()
examples.description: JSX compiles to React.createElement(). React decides whether to create a DOM element, function component, or class component. Factory pattern in the frontend.
examples.lesson: Even React's JSX is Factory pattern. createElement() is the ultimate frontend Factory.
